case_studies.title: Michael Burry's Housing Short (2005-2008)

thesis.contrarian_insight: Subprime mortgages would default en masse, bringing down the housing market
thesis.research: Analyzed thousands of individual mortgage pools
thesis.position: Bought credit default swaps on subprime MBS
thesis.outcome: Made $750M for his fund, ~100x return on premium paid

timeline.2006-2007: Faced investor redemptions and margin calls

lessons: Contrarian positions require conviction to hold through adversity
Deep research can uncover what consensus misses
Asymmetric bets can deliver extraordinary returns
Being early is painful but can be worth it

case_studies.title: Templeton's WWII Buying Spree (1939)

thesis.contrarian_insight: War would boost industrial production and end Depression
thesis.position: Bought $100 of every NYSE stock trading under $1 (104 companies)
thesis.timing: Started buying days after Germany invaded Poland

lessons: Buying when others are panic selling
Long-term economic growth overcomes short-term crises

case_studies.title: Oaktree's 2008-2009 Distressed Buying

thesis.contrarian_insight: Market panic created unprecedented distressed opportunities
thesis.action: Deployed $3.5B in distressed debt at 50-60 cents on dollar
thesis.memos: 'Now It's Time to Buy' memo went out September 2008
thesis.outcome: Returned 55% in 2009, significant alpha vs. market

lessons: Act decisively when sentiment reaches extremes
Second-level thinking: 'Everyone knows it's bad, but is it priced in?'
Discipline to buy when everything feels wrong
